mentions.note: Trade-down retail theme; financial stress driving shift from full-price to discount retail.
Rival off-price retailer down 2.2%; underperformance signals consumer weakness ahead.
Had best numbers of entire discount retail group yet among worst S&P 500 performers today; head-scratcher; should be bought on weakness.
Strong off-price execution benefiting from consumer trade-down; TJX and Ross separating via superior management execution in weak consumer environment.

Off-price retail leader up 47% under CEO Jim Conroy. 17% comp growth, 20% revenue growth, 27% earnings growth despite consumer pressure. Better merchandising, marketing, in-store experience driving outperformance. Runway to 3,600 stores from 2,300. Absolutely worth owning; off-price one of few working retail segments.
